section.name: BreakTest Features And Differences
section.p: This is the BreakTest manual. It documents the features and behavioral changes that are specific to BreakTest 2026.07.11. For the inherited JMeter-compatible test-plan model, use the official Apache JMeter documentation.

ul.li: Java 21 or later is required.
Virtual threads are used where they reduce thread overhead.
Legacy BeanShell, BSF/JEXL2, Rhino JavaScript, and LogKit paths were removed from the modern scripting/runtime surface.
JMeter-compatible names are retained where they are part of file formats, APIs, plugins, scripts, or automation.
The built-in HTTP sampler path uses Apache HttpClient 5.
HTTP/2 and HTTP/2-preferred protocol selection are available through the async HttpClient 5 path.
BreakTest handles negotiated fallback, HTTP/2 upload content length, connect-time reporting, and safer replay behavior.
HTTP/2 resource usage was reduced by limiting reactor threads and avoiding unnecessary client/reactor rebuilds between same-user iterations.
NTLM authentication was fixed on the HttpClient 5 path, including variable resolution before async authentication callbacks.
Brotli and Zstandard response decoding were added alongside gzip and deflate.
Lazy response decompression stores compressed bytes and decompresses only when response data is accessed.
Response retention modes can keep compressed data, discard successful bodies, keep only failing bodies, or retain MD5 checksums.
Lightweight test-plan cloning reduces per-thread object churn.
Header merging, temporary property handling, response read buffers, and diagnostic metadata paths were optimized.
Rich sample diagnostics are lazy and listener-driven, so normal load runs avoid unnecessary per-sample storage.

section.p: BreakTest moves load modeling into a unified Thread Group rather than splitting common scenarios across several specialized thread groups.

ul.li: Closed-model schedules can combine different ramp-up speeds, hold periods, and target thread counts.
Open-model schedules can model arrivals, use even-arrival helpers, and cap active threads.
Thread Group pacing controls are available for transaction pacing.
Schedule parsing is safer and includes graph preview support.
Runtime user variables are cleared on new iterations when same-user mode is disabled, while initial variables are preserved.
Pause and Resume are available in the GUI and through bin/pause.* and bin/resume.*.
In closed model, pause holds active threads and stops further ramp-up.
In open model, pause stops new arrivals and lets active threads finish naturally, so load drops while paused.
Shutdown no longer waits for timers to complete; it waits for in-flight server responses to finish cleanly.
Parallel Controller runs child samplers concurrently with bounded scheduling.
Fork Controller starts asynchronous child branches that share virtual-user context and complete before thread teardown.
HTTP state handling was tightened to reduce unsafe sharing in concurrent paths.

section.p: BreakTest includes local agent and MCP support for AI-assisted script repair. Tools such as Codex, Claude Code, and opencode can inspect a JMX script, validate it, repair it, and show changes in the GUI.

ul.li: Create or repair extractors and correlations.
Parameterize dynamic values.
Add assertions.
Fix selectors and unstable recorded data.
Review generated changes from the GUI before continuing.
Cleaner light/dark GUI styling.
Undo and redo for semantic tree edits including add, delete, update, move, and search/replace operations.
Background JMX loading with a loading overlay.
Optional fast GUI loading through breaktest.gui.load.fast=true.
Missing plugin elements load as disabled placeholders instead of failing the whole file.
Search Tree can flag pre-processors, post-processors, assertions, timers, and config elements.
Search results can be removed selectively with confirmation.
View Results Tree shows richer request, response, endpoint, HTTP/TLS, cookie, variable, and binary/text diagnostics.
Jump-to-source support connects samples back to test elements.
BreakTest JMX metadata preserves origin and source-path details.
When BreakTest HAR metadata is present, recorded request/response tabs are shown in View Results Tree and HTTP sampler editors.
Recorded-vs-replayed diffs help debug HAR migration and HTTP/2 replay.
Transaction child samples preserve source paths so folded transaction results can still navigate to the correct source element.
If and While Controllers support structured condition rows with all/any matching while preserving legacy expressions.
Loop and while index behavior options are visible and configurable.
Transaction Controller includes measurement modes plus delay and pacing controls.
Boundary, CSS/HTML, Regex, XPath, XPath2, JSONPath, and JMESPath extractors support fail-on-no-match behavior.
CSV Data Set can read records in random order.
CSV preview shows first sample variable assignments.
UDP Request sends datagrams with optional response waiting, response timeout, local address and port binding, and socket reuse.
UDP Receiver can read a later datagram from a named socket created by UDP Request.
A friendly payload-format selector provides UTF-8 text, hexadecimal, and single-byte text; advanced custom codecs can implement the native UDPTrafficCodec interface.
The implementation uses the Java networking API directly and does not require the JMeter Plugins common I/O or common JMeter libraries.

section.p: See Native UDP Request And Receiver for configuration details.

ul.li: RMI Remote Server style distributed execution is removed. Use BreakTest Enterprise or another controlled orchestration layer for distributed runs.
BreakTest-specific JMX metadata may not round-trip into older Apache JMeter versions.
Plugins that depend on removed internals may require migration.
HttpClient 4 runtime jars remain available for legacy plugin compatibility, but the built-in HTTP Request sampler uses HttpClient 5.
